title: lathyrus pauciflorus subsp. utahensis
intro: Lathyrus pauciflorus subsp. utahensis is a sub-species of the pea family (Fabaceae) native to the mountainous regions of North America, specifically found in the western United States and parts of Canada, characterized by its delicate purple flowers and slender stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Lathyrus pauciflorus subsp. utahensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for a range of applications in natural health remedies.
